Fazail.text: The Holy Qur'an has declared four months as sacred, one of them is 'Muharram al-Haram' (Al-Tawbah 36:9).
The Prophet ﷺ called Muharram as Shahr-Allah (the month of Allah). No other month has this honor. (Muslim: 1163)
* The decree of the Prophet ﷺ is: 'After Ramadan, the best fasts are those of Allah in the month of Muharram.' (Muslim: 1163)

The lunar year begins with Muharram. The names of the lunar months have been used since the time of Jahiliyyah and no name has been changed, except for Muharram. His name was 'Safar I', which was changed to 'Muharram'.

Amaal.text: Post-partum fasts should be arranged frequently in Muharram. Fasting on the day of Ashura (10, 9 Muharram) should be observed. By this, the sins of the previous year are forgiven. (Muslim: 1162)

Rasm.text: Considering Muharram as a month of mourning and avoiding marriage and other joyous activities in it. Spending generously on family members in the month of Muharram with the intention that Allah will bless him throughout the year. Repairing the graves, reciting Fatiha on them and offering flowers and offerings. On the day of Ashura, performing ta'zia, honoring it and cooking and distributing it. In the first ten days of Muharram, organizing or participating in funeral processions and funerals, as well as offering milk and sweet water sables. Wearing black clothes as mourning and dressing children in green clothes as fakirs of Hazrat Hussain. Considering the Dhul-Jinah (horse) as blessed, kissing it, passing oneself and children under it and participating in its procession with the intention of reward. All these actions are prohibited by the Qur'an and Sunnah.

Waqiat.text: On the 10th of Muharram 1824 BC, Pharaoh Munftah drowned and Allah saved Hazrat Musa (peace be upon him) and his people from his persecution.
In Muharram, 10th of Prophethood, the cruel scripture regarding the siege of Muslims in Shaab-e-Abi Talib was written.
In Muharram 3 AH / June 624 A.D., the attack of Dhi Amr took place.
In Muharram 3 AH / June 624 AD, Sayyidah Umm Kulthum, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah (PBUH), was married to Hazrat Uthman (RA).
On 1 Muharram 7 AH/11 May 628 AD, the Prophet ﷺ sent letters to various kings and princes.
On 1 Muharram 7 AH / May 628 AD, the Khyber invasion took place.
In Muharram 9 AH / April 630, the Prophet ﷺ sent servants to collect Zakat.
In Muharram 12 AH / March, April 633 AD, the Battle of Zaat-ul-Salsil was fought between Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed (RA) and the Iranian governor of Ubla, Hurmuz.
In Muharram 17 AH/January 638 AD, Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqqas (RA) founded the city of Kufa during the caliphate of Hazrat Omar (RA).

In Muharram 18 AH/January 639, an epidemic of plague broke out in Amwas (Palestine), from which many companions of Khalid bin Waleed died, including Hazrat Abu Ubaidah bin Jarrah, Mu'adh bin Jabal, Yazid bin Abi Sufyan, Shahrabeel bin Hasna (RA).

On 16 Muharram 19 AH / 15 January 640 AD, Nahavand was conquered. Hazrat Nu'man bin Muqrin, may Allah be pleased with him, led 15,000 Muslims and defeated the Jam Ghafir of 150,000 Iranians and was martyred.
In Muharram 22 AH/December 642 AD, Hazrat Amr Bin Asas (RA) settled a city called "Fustat" and declared it the capital of Egypt instead of Alexandria.
On 1st Muharram 24 AH / 4 November 644 AD, the second caliph Hazrat Umar bin Khattab (RA) died and the third caliph Hazrat Uthman bin Affan (RA) succeeded to the caliphate.
In Muharram 31 AH/August 651 AD, a pin miller killed the last Sassanid king, Yazdgerd, and threw his body into the river.
In Muharram 37 AH / June 657 AD, the battle of "Safin" took place between Hazrat Ali (RA) and Hazrat Amir Mu'awiya (RA).
On 10 Muharram 61 AH / 1 October 680 AD, Hazrat Hussain (RA) was martyred at Karbala.
On 22 Muharram 169 AH / 5 August 785 AD, Abbasid Caliph Mahdi bin Mansur died and his son Hadi bin Mahdi ascended the throne.
On 5 Muharram 198 AH / 25 September 813 AD, Abbasid Caliph Amin al-Rashid was killed and Mamun al-Rashid ascended the throne.
In Muharram 236 AH/July 850 AD, Caliph Mutawakkil Ali Allah forbade people to visit the shrine of Hazrat Hussain (RA) and erased all traces of Karbala.

In Muharram 278 AH/April 891 AD, a man named Hamdan Qurmat founded the "Qarmat religion" in the land of Kufa. This person believed in seven imams and considered Muhammad bin Hanafiyah bin Ali bin Abi Talib as a messenger.
In Muharram 352 AH / January 963, Mu'azdullah initiated the lamentation, mourning and ceremony of Muharram.
In Muharram 378 AH / April 988, the world's largest observatory was built in Baghdad during the reign of Sharaf al-Dawla Bayhi.

In Muharram 500 AH / September 1106 AH, Amirul Muslimin Yusuf bin Tashfin was called after ruling Andalus for 45 years. He had increased Islamic power in Andalusia by defeating Alphonso VI in the Battle of Zalaqah (579 AH/1086 AD).
On 1 Muharram 699 AH / 25 September 1299 AD, the Ottoman Empire began with the accession of Turkoman Sardar Usman Khan to the throne in Anatolia.
On 28 Muharram 1394 AH / 22 February 1974, the Second Islamic Summit Conference was held in Lahore.
27 Muharram 1420 AH / 13 May 1999 Thursday, Grand Mufti of Saudi Arabia Sheikh Abdul Aziz bin Baz, may God have mercy on him, passed away.

Fazail.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: “No disease is contagious (without the will of God), it is not (permissible) to take bad omens, there is no reality of the owl (or the call of the spirit) and the noise of the moon.” (Bukhari: 5705)

Amaal.text: Nowhere in the Qur'an and Hadith, any good deed in the month of Safar has been singled out with virtue, so the Masnoon deeds that are done on normal days should also be done in this month.

Rasm.text: Considering the month of Safar as inauspicious, especially its first thirteen (13) days. These people call them Thirteen Tazi. Do not marry in this month due to bad luck, as well as breaking earthenware and old vessels. On the last Wednesday of Safar (Wednesday, Saturday), celebrating "Health Bath Day", distributing sweets, taking out processions and wearing amulets or chullas etc. for the recovery of patients and performing the ritual of stealing. All these actions are prohibited by the Qur'an and Sunnah.

Waqiat.text: On 12 Safar 2 AH / 4 August 623 A.D., the battle with the infidels was permitted by divine revelation.
In Safar 2 AH / August 623 A.D., the invasion of "Abuwa" or "Widdan" took place.
Safar 4 AH / July 625 A.D. The tragedies of "Raji and Bir Ma'una" took place.

On 1 Safar 12 AH / 17 April 633 AD, the battle of Madhar (Iraq) was fought between Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed (RA) and the Iranian general Qaran bin Quryans, in which the Iranians were defeated and 30 thousand Iranians were killed.
On 16 Safar 15 AH / 30 March 636 AD, Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqqas (RA) along with his army descended on Qadisiya.

On 26 Safar 16 AH / 29 March 637 AD, Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqqas (RA) conquered Mada'in. He placed a pulpit in Kasra's Qasrabeed (White House) and offered Friday prayers. It was the first Friday on this land.
Hamdan (Iran) was conquered on 25 Safar 20 AH / 11 February 641 AD.

On 25 Safar 20 AH / 11 February 641 AD, Hazrat Amr bin As, may God bless him and grant him peace, conquered the Egyptian fort "Firma" and on the same day Caesar of Rome Heracles died in Constantinople.
On 1st Safar 99 AH / 13 September 717 AD, the Umayyad Caliph Sulaiman bin Abdul Malik died and his brother-in-law Hazrat Umar bin Abdul Aziz, may God have mercy on him, succeeded to the caliphate.

In Safar 180 AH/April 796 AD, Emir of Andalus Hisham bin Abdul Rahman I died and his son Huqam bin Hisham ascended the throne. In the place "Wasit" (Iraq) the land was divided, due to which 1300 villages were buried in the ground.
On 2 Safar 366 AH / 29 September 976 A.D., Amir Al-Andalus Hukm II died and his son Hisham II ascended the throne.
On 1 Safar 589 AH / 6 February 1193 AD, Sultan Salahuddin Ayubi, may Allah have mercy on him, died in Damascus.

On 9 Safar 656 AH / 7 February 1258 AH, Genghis Khan's grandson Halaku Khan entered Baghdad. He killed the Abbasid caliph Mustasim Bullah and ended the Abbasid caliphate, 16 million Muslims were killed in Baghdad and its surroundings by his army.
On 19-17 Safar 886 AH / 19-17 April 1481, Fatih Constantinople Sultan Muhammad Fatih Osmani, may Allah have mercy on him, died after thirty years of rule.
On 7 Safar 918 AH / 24 April 1512 AD, the Ottoman Caliph Sultan Salim I succeeded to the Musand Sultanate.
In Safar 1034 AH / November 1624, Sheikh Ahmad Sirhandi died in Sirhind. He is called Mujaddid al-Thani.
On 27 Safar 1037 AH / 28 October 1627 AD, Mughal Emperor Ruknuddin Muhammad Jahangir died and was buried in Shahdara (Lahore). Jahangir ruled for 22 years.
On Safar 1274 AH / 21 September 1857, Delhi was captured by the British and the last Mughal emperor Sirajuddin Bahadur Shah Zafar was arrested and sent to Rangoon.
Shah Faisal bin Abdul Aziz Al Saud was born in Safar 1324 AH / April 1906.
In Safar 1333 AH / December 1914, Maulana Altaf Hussain Hali, the famous Urdu poet and author of Masnavi Madd wa Jazar Islam, died.
On 13 Safar 1359 AH / 23 March 1940, a separate country (Pakistan) was demanded for Muslims in the Lahore Resolution.

On 27 Safar 1387 AH / 6 June 1967, the third Arab-Israeli war began. In this six-day war, Israel took Bethlehem and West Jordan from Jordan, the Sinai Peninsula and Raghaza from Egypt, and the Golan from Syria.

On 7 Safar 1400 AH / 27 December 1979, Russia attacked Afghanistan. As a result of the death of thousands of Russian soldiers and the martyrdom of 10 lakh Afghans, the Russian army left Afghanistan on February 15, 1989, humiliated and humiliated.
On 1 Safar 1419 AH / 28 May 1998, Pakistan became the first Islamic nuclear power by detonating 5 nuclear weapons.

Fazail.text: There is no clarity about the greatness and excellence of this month from Quran and Hadith. The traditions that are narrated in this regard are unauthentic.

Amaal.text: There is no text in the Qur'an and Hadith about the virtue of doing any work in this month, nor is there anything related to it from the Companions, so the good deeds that are done on normal days should also be done in this month.

Rasm.text: Seeing the moon of Rabi-ul-Awal, saying "Eid Mubarak" to people or announcing the arrival of Eid. To keep holding Milad festivals throughout the month and to stand in reverence while waiting for the arrival of Mustafa ﷺ. Declaring the 12th of Rabi-ul-Awal as the "third Eid" and considering it more important than the two Eids appointed by Allah and the Messenger. Also celebrating the festival and taking out or participating in processions in the form of buses, cars, trolleys and bullock carts. Displaying flags at various places and reciting self-made exaggerated chants. Making models of the Kaaba, Rouza Mubaraka, Masjid Naburi and other holy places and circumambulating them and organizing mixed and non-mixed gatherings of fireworks, dancing and singing. All these actions are prohibited by the Qur'an and Sunnah.

Waqiat.date: 9 Rabi Al-Awwal (according to Rajih Qul) Aam al-Fail 20 or 22 April 571 AD
Waqiat.text: 9 Rabi al-Awwal (according to Rajih Qul) on Monday 20th or 22nd of April 571 A.D. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was born happily.
On the 8th of Rabi al-Awwal, 1st Hijri / 20th of Timur 622 AD, the Prophet ﷺ descended to the Quba, laid the foundation of the Quba Mosque and taught the first Friday.
On 19 Rabi Al-Awwal 1 Hijri / 1 October 622, the Prophet ﷺ laid the foundation of Masjid Nabawi in Mohalla Bani Salem.
In Rabi al-Awwal 2 AH / September 623 AD, the Prophet ﷺ accompanied 200 Companions in pursuit of a caravan of Quraysh and went to the place "Bawat".
In Rabi al-Awwal 2 AH / September 623 AD, the Battle of Safwan, i.e. the Battle of Badr, took place. The Prophet ﷺ followed Kurz bin Jabir Fahri with 70 Companions and went to Sufwan.
In Rabi al-Awwal 4 AH / August 625 AD, the Jewish tribe Banunazir was exiled from Medina.
On 25 Rabi al-Awwal 5 AH / 31 July 626 AD, the Prophet ﷺ along with 1000 companions attacked a city called Dumat al-Jandal, which is known as the Battle of Dumat al-Jandal.
In Rabi al-Awwal 7 AH / July 628 AD (according to Rajih Qul) the Zat al-Raqqa' raid took place.
On Monday, 12 Rabi-ul-Awal 11 AH / 9 June 632 AH, the Prophet ﷺ met Rafiq-e-Ala.
In Rabi al-Awwal 11 AH / June 632 AD, Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddiq (RA) was nominated as the first Caliph of the Muslims.
In Rabi al-Awwal 12 AH/June 633 AD, Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed (RA) attacked the city of Hira in Iraq and conquered it without a fight.
On 25 Rabi al-Awwal 13 AH / 30 May 634 AD, Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed (RA) conquered the city of Busra in Syria.
On 23 Rabi al-Awwal 15 AH / 5 May 636 AD, Hazrat Abu Ubaidah (RA) conquered Baalbak (Lebanon).
On 25 Rabi al-Awwal 15 AH / 7 May 636 AD, Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed under the command of Hazrat Abu Ubaidah (RA) conquered the important city of Syria "Homs".
In Rabi al-Awwal 41 AH / July 661 AD, peace was made between Hazrat Hasan and Hazrat Muawiyah (RA) and Hazrat Muawiyah (RA) unanimously became the Caliph of the Muslims.

On 14 Rabi al-Awwal 170 AH / 31 August 786 AD, the Abbasid Caliph Hadi bin Mahdi died and his brother Harun al-Rashid ascended the throne. On the same day, Haroon al-Rashid's son Mamun al-Rashid was born. It is a strange coincidence that on the same day one Caliph died, another abdicated and a third was born.
On 20 Rabi al-Awwal 227 AH / 19 December 841 AD, the Abbasid Caliph Mutasim Allah died and the power passed to his son Harun Wasiq Allah.
Imam Ahmad bin Hanbal, may God have mercy on him, died in Rabi al-Awwal 241 AH / July 855 AH.

In Rabi al-Awwal 300 AH / October 912 AH, Amir-e-Andalus Abdullah died and his grandson Abd al-Rahman III ascended the throne. Abdul Rahman ruled for fifty years and fought jihad every year. His era is known as the golden age of Andalusia.
Sultan Alp Arslan Faljuqi died on 10 Rabi Al-Awwal 465 AH / 15 November 1072 AD and his son Malik Shah ascended the throne.
On 22 Rabi al-Awwal 886 AH / 23 May 1481 AD, the rulership of the Ottoman Empire came to the hands of Bayezid II. During his reign, Maldives, Croatia and Slovenia were conquered.
On 12 Rabi Al-Awwal 897 AH / 2 January 1492 AD, Granada fell and Muslim rule from Andalus came to an end.
On 21 Rabi al-Awwal 923 AH / 13 April 1517 AD, Egypt came under the control of the Ottoman government.
Nuruddin Jahangir, the fourth king of the Mughal dynasty, was born on 17 Rabi Al-Awwal 977 AH / 31 August 1569.
In Rabi al-Awwal 1349 AH / July 1930, Qazi Syed Sulaiman Mansoor Puri, may Allah have mercy on him, died in a ship while returning from Hajj.
In Rabi Al-Awwal 1373 AH / November 1953, Syed Sulaiman Nadvi, may Allah have mercy on him, died in Karachi.

Fazail.text: There is no merit of this month in Quran and Hadith.

Amaal.text: No masnoon action of this month is described in the hadith and no concept and custom of the Arabs is also transmitted.

Rasm.text: Celebrating "Eleventh Sharif" on the eleventh and seventeenth of this month. Taking out or participating in the eleventh procession. Cooking pots and distributing them for the reward of Sheikh Abdul Qadir Jilani, may God have mercy on him. Facing Baghdad and reciting "Salaat-i-Ghosiya" and exaggerating the glory of Sheikh Abdul Qadir Jilani, may God have mercy on him, and asking for help from him. All these actions are prohibited by the Qur'an and Sunnah.

Waqiat.text: In Rabi al-Thani 3 AH / September 624 AD, the Messenger of Allah, peace be upon him, along with 300 companions, went to a place called "Bahran". This is called Ghazwa-e-Barisan.
In Rabi al-Thani 6 AH / August 627 AD, the Prophet ﷺ sent Hazrat Abu Ubaidah bin Jarrah (RA) along with 40 people to Dhul-Qasa.
In Rabi al-Thani 6 AH / August 627 AD, Hazrat Zayd bin Haritha Radiyallahu Anhu was made the Amir of Syria and sent to Jamum.
On 1 Rabi al-Thani 11 AH / 26 June 632 AD, Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddique (Radi Allahu Anhu) sent the army of Usama towards "Muta".

On the 21st of Rabi al-Thani 15 AH/2 June 636 AD, Amirul Momineen Hazrat Umar Farooq (RA) wrote a letter to Hazrat Abu Ubaidah (RA) asking him to stop the series of conquests in Syria until I reconsider my opinion.
On 21st Rabi al-Thani 15 AH / 2nd June 636 AD, the people of Homs surrendered the city of Homs to Khalid bin Waleed (Radi Allahu Anhu) on the condition of Jizya and Aman.

In Rabi al-Thani 16 AH/May 637 AD, peace was made between Hazrat Umar bin Khattab and the bishop of Jerusalem (Bait al-Maqdis) Safar and Nuys, and he handed over the keys of the city to Hazrat Umar, thus Bait al-Maqdis was conquered.
On 7th Rabi' al-Thani 20 AH / 24 March 641 AD, Hazrat Amr bin As (RA) conquered Bilbis (Egypt).
On 15 Rabi al-Thani 22 AH / 21 March 642 CE, Cyrus the Great (former king of Egypt) died in Alexandria.
On 12 Rabi' al-Thani 172 AH/June 796 AD, Abdul Rahman Dakhil died in Andalus and his son Hisham ascended the throne.
On the 7th of Rabi al-Thani 179 AH / 1st July 795 AD, Imam Malik, may God have mercy on him, died in Madinah.
In Rabi al-Thani 238 AH / September 852 AD, the Amir of Andalus Abdul Rahman II died and his son Muhammad ascended the throne.
On 15 Rabi al-Thani 248 AH / June 862, the Abbasid Caliph Muntasir Bullah died and the leadership of the Caliphate came to Mustain Bullah.

Sultan Mahmud of Ghaznavi died in Rabi al-Thani 421 AH / April 1030 AD. Mahmud of Ghaznavi wrested the territories of Sarhad, Punjab and Sindh from the Hindu kings and defeated the united forces of the great kings of India in the Battle of Somnath (Thewar of Gujarat) (1026 AD).
Sheikh Abdul Qadir Jilani, may Allah have mercy on him, died on 1 Rabi al-Thani 561 AH / 4 Farvi 1166 AH.
On 23 Rabi al-Thani 974 AH / December 1566, Ottoman Sultan Suleiman the Righteous died after ruling for 46 years.
On 8 Rabi al-Thani 978 AH / 9 September 1570 AD, the city of Lefkosa (Nicosia) in Cyprus was conquered.
On 10 Rabi al-Thani 979 AH / 1 September 1571, Maghusa (Fama Gusta) was conquered.
On 10th Rabi'ul-Thani 979 AH / 1st September 1571 AD, Sultan Salim II completely conquered "Cyprus".

Bahadur Shah Zafar, the last Mughal emperor, died in Rangoon (Myanmar) on 20 Rabi-ul-Thani 1279 AH / 15 October 1862. After the British recaptured Delhi in January 1857 (September 1857), Bahadur Shah Zafar was arrested and sent to prison.
Mirza Ghulam Ahmad Qadiani, the false claimant of prophethood, died on 24 Rabi'ul-Thani 1326 AH/27 May 1908.

Fazail.text: There is no mention in the Qur'an and Hadith about the excellence of this month.

Amaal.text: In the Quran and Hadith, no special virtues have been mentioned about this month, so the Masnoon deeds that are done in other days should also be done in this month.

Rasm.text: We do not have any special customs related to this month, that is, this month is free from illegal rituals and myths.

Waqiat.text: 170 people under the command of Hazrat Zayd bin Haritha Radiyallahu Anhu were sent to ``Aes'' in 6 AH / September 627 AD. This event is known by the name of "Zayd bin Haritha".

Waqiat.date: Jami Awwala 8 AH / August or September 629 AD
Waqiat.text: A bloody battle took place between the Muslims and the Romans at the place of "Muta" in Jami Awwala 8 AH / August or September 629 AD. In this battle, the number of Muslims was 3000 while the Romans were 200,000. In this, 12 Companions were martyred while countless Romans went to hell and 9 swords were broken by Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed.

On 2 Jumadi al-Awwala 13 AH/29 July 634 AD, a battle took place between the Muslims and the Romans at the place of "Ajnadin" (Palestine). The number of Romans was more than one hundred thousand while the number of Muslims was 33000 thousand. The Romans were defeated and 30,000 thousand Romans were killed.
In Jumadi al-Awwala 16 AH / June 637 AD, Hazrat Abdullah bin Mu'tam, a talented commander of the army of Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqqas (RA) conquered Tikrit (Iraq).

On 15 Jumadi al-Awwala 20 AH / 30 April 641 AD, the Battle of Ain Al-Shams (Egypt) was fought. The Islamic army consisted of 12,000 people, whose leader was Hazrat Amr bin As, while the Byzantine (Roman) army was 20,000. The Byzantines were defeated and only 300 Byzantines managed to escape.
Hazrat Abdullah bin Zubair (RA) was martyred on 1st Jumadi Al-Awwala 73 AH / 18 September 692 AD.

In Jumadi al-Awli 220 AH/May 835, Caliph Mutasim Bullah Abbasi established a military camp on the banks of the Tigris, 90 miles north of Baghdad, built his palace, built houses for the army, built a market, a mosque, and settled the Turks, and named the newly rebuilt city "Suraman Rayi", which became known as Samara in the same year, instead of Baghdad. made

In Jumadi al-Awli 359 AH/March 970, the vizier Johar of the Ubayd (Fatimi) Caliph Mu'az captured Egypt, went to Jama'i Ibn Tulun and offered prayers and ordered the addition of "Hai Ali Khair al-Amal" to the call to prayer.
Sultan Mahmud of Ghaznavi ascended the throne in Jamadi-ul-Awla 389 AH/April 998.
Qutbuddin Aibak, the first Muslim king of India and the founder of the Ghulam dynasty, died in Jumadi-ul-Awla 607 AH/October 1210.

On 6 Jumadi al-Awwala 719 AH / June 1319, the Governor of Granada, Abu Waleed Ismail, defeated more than 2 lakh Christians with an army of 5,500. One lakh Christians were killed in this war while only 13 Muslims were martyred.

On 2 Jumadi al-Awli 726 AH / 16 April 1326, the second Ottoman Sultan Urkhan conquered the city of Bursa (Turkey). Ur Khan was the first Ottoman ruler to expand the Islamic Empire in Eastern Europe by crossing the Danyal Pass.

On 20 Jumadi Awwala 857 AH / 29 May 1453 AD, Sultan Muhammad Conquered Constantinople and named it Islambul (Fortress of Islam). Sultan Muhammad attacked with an army of 100,000 and after 54 days of fighting, the city was conquered. This is how the Roman Empire, which lasted for more or less one and a half thousand years, came to an end.
On 15 Jumadi Al-Awla 974 AH/ 30 September 1560 AD, the Ottoman Caliph Salim II succeeded to the caliphate.
On Jumadi Awwala 1365 AH / 17 April 1946, the French troops left Syria forever and Syria was liberated.
Fateh Qadian Maulana Sanaullah Amritsari died in 1367 AH/March 1948.

Fazail.text: There is no explanation about the greatness and excellence of this month from both Quran and Hadith.

Amaal.text: In the Qur'an and Hadith, no action has been singled out with excellence in relation to this month, so the Masnoon actions that are performed on normal days should also be performed in this month.

Rasm.text: We do not have any special custom in relation to this month, that is, this month is also free from illegal customs and myths.

Waqiat.text: In Jumadi al-Thania 3 AH / November 624, the Prophet ﷺ sent a group of 100 men under the leadership of Hazrat Zayd bin Haritha, whose target was the Quraysh trade caravan.
In Jumadi al-Thania 6 AH / October 627, the decisive battle of Nineveh in Iraq took place between Rome and Iran, in which the Romans prevailed.
In Jumadi al-Thania 7 AH / October 628 AD, the Prophet ﷺ made Hazrat Zayd bin Haritha the Ameer of Syria with 500 men and sent him to "Hasmia".

Waqiat.date: Jumadi al-Thania 8 AH / September or October 629 AD
Waqiat.text: In Jumadi al-Thania 8 AH / September or October 629 AD, the Prophet ﷺ made Hazrat Amr bin As, Radiyallahu Anhu, the Ameer of Syria and sent him to Zaat-ul-Salsil.
On 7 Jumadi al-Thania 14 AH / 8 August 634 AD, Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddiq (RA) fell ill.
On 17 Jumadi al-Thania 13 AH / 22 August 634 AD, Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed (RA) defeated Durnjar Rumi in Marj al-Safar (Syria).
On 21st Jumadi al-Thania 13 AH/27th August 634 AD, Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddiq (RA) died and Hazrat Umar Farooq (RA) succeeded to the post of caliphate.
In Jumadi al-Thania 21 AH / May 641 AD, the commander of Islam, Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed, may God be pleased with him, died.
On 10 Jumadi al-Thania 36 AH / 5 December 656 AD, the "Battle of Jamal" took place between the armies of Hazrat Ali and Hazrat Aisha. Hazrat Talha and Zubair were martyred in this battle.

In Jumadi al-Thania 92 AH/March 712 AD, Muhammad bin Qasim, may Allah have mercy on him, attacked Debal (Sindh). He conquered areas as far as Multan and Gujarat (India) and laid the foundations of Islamic rule in the subcontinent.
Caliph Haroon al-Rashid died on 3 Jumadi al-Thaniya 193 AH / 24 March 809 AD after ruling for 23 years and two and a half months.
In Jumadi al-Thania 510 AH / December 1174, Sultan Salahuddin Ayyubi captured the whole of Syria.

On 16 Jumadi al-Thaniyyah 923 AH / 6 July 1517, Muhammad Abunami, the son of the Emir of Makkah Barakat II, handed over the keys of Makkah, Madinah and the Kaaba to Sultan Salim I, and Hijaz became part of the Ottoman Empire.

Mughal Emperor Akbar died in Jamadi-ul-Thania 1014 AH / October 1605 and his son Jahangir ascended the throne. Akbar Chitta was illiterate, ascended the throne at the age of thirteen and ruled for fifty years and extended the Mughal Empire to Kashmir, Gujarat, Kathiawar and Rahmadnagar (Deccan).
On the death of Akbar Shah II in 1253 AH / September 1837, his son Bahadur Shah Zafar, the last Mughal king, took over the reigns.
Nawab Siddique Hasan Khan, may Allah bless him and grant him peace, died in Jamadi Al-Thania 1307 AH / January 1890.
In Jumadi al-Thania 1364 AH / May 1945, the Second World War ended in Europe.
A general referendum was held on 1st Jumadi al-Thaniya 1375 AH / 15 January 1956, in which the people of Sudan claimed the right to complete independence.
On 6 Jumadi al-Thania 1390 AH / 21 August 1969, a fire was burning in Al-Aqsa Mosque for four hours due to the mischief of a Jew, from which a part of the south-western side of the mosque collapsed.
In Jumadi al-Thania 1408 AH/January 1988, Dar al-Salaam, an international organization for the publication of the Book and Sunnah, started its publishing work in Riyadh (Saudi Arabia).
On 22 Jumadi al-Thania 1420 AH / 3 October 1999, Sheikh Nasir al-Din al-Albani, may God have mercy on him, died.

Fazail.text: No special virtue of the month of Rajab is mentioned in the Qur'an and Hadith, except that it is one of the four sacred months. All the traditions that are mentioned about the virtues of the month of Rajab are weak, not even one of them is authentic. It is known that the Prophet ﷺ ascended in this month, but there is no clarity about this from the Qur'an, Hadith and the Companions.

Amaal.text: In this month no good deed is distinguished with virtue. Some of the actions which are exaggerated with virtue are just fictitious stories and stories based on weak and subjective traditions, so the actions which are proven by the Book and Sunnah should be followed.

Rasm.text: In the month of Rajab, keeping three fasts on Thursday, Friday and Saturday, as well as praying 12 rakat prayers in a specific manner from Maghrib to Isha on Friday night. Reading "Salaat al-Ragheeb" on the night of the first Friday of Rajab. To organize frequent post-natal fasts and to give Zakat in the same month with the intention of increasing reward and reward. 22. To perform the ritual of Konda on 22 Rajab. On the 27th night of Rajab, performing special worship, lighting lamps at mosques and organizing events and festivals on the occasion of the "Night of Ascension". Celebrating the 27th of Rajab as the Day of Ascension and fasting on that day, organizing meetings and processions, lighting fireworks and candles. All these actions are prohibited by the Qur'an and Sunnah.

Waqiat.text: The first migration to Abyssinia took place in Rajab 5 of the Prophet, in which Hazrat Uthman and Ruqayyah, Zubair, Abd al-Rahman bin Awf, Ibn Mas'ud and Abu Salama and Wam Salama (RA) also participated.
According to Rajih Qul, Abu Talib, the uncle of the Prophet (peace be upon him) died in Rajab 10 Prophet/April 619 AD.
In Rajab 2 AH / January 624 AD, the Prophet ﷺ made Hazrat Abdullah bin Jahsh the Amir of Syria and sent him to Nakhla with 12 emigrants.
On 1 Rajab 9 AH / 14 October 630 AH, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ left for Tabuk with 30,000 companions.
King of Abyssinia Najashi died in Rajab 9 AH / November 630 AD and the Prophet ﷺ offered his funeral prayer in absentia.
On 4th Rajab 12 AH / 14 September 633 AD, Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed (RA) conquered the region of Anbar in Iraq.
On 11 Rajab 12 AH / 21 September 633 AD, Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed (RA) captured Ain al-Tamar (Iraq).
On 24 Rajab 12 AH / 5 October 633 AD, Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed (Radi Allahu Anhu) conquered Dumat al-Jandal.
On 26 Rajab 13 AH / 25 September 634 AD, Umar appointed Abu Ubaidah as commander of the army in place of Khalid bin Waleed.
On 15 Rajab 14 AH / 5 September 635 AD, Hazrat Abu Ubaidah (RA) conquered Damascus.

On 5 Rajab 15 AH / 13 August 636 AD, the decisive battle of Yarmouk took place. In this battle, the number of Romans was 2 lakh and the number of Muslims was 33 thousand. The Romans were defeated and more than 124 thousand Romans were killed.
On the death of Amir Mu'awiya (RA) on Thursday, 22 Rajab 60 AH / 17 April 680 AD, Yazid took over the government.
On 5 Rajab 92 AH / 28 April 711 AD, Tariq bin Ziyad left for Spain (Andalus) from Tangier to conquer Andalus.

In Rajab 141 AH / November 758, Abbasid Caliph Abu Ja'far Mansur founded the city of Baghdad. The construction of this city continued for almost 8 years and its construction was completed in 149 AH/766 AD.
Hazrat Jafar Sadiq, may God have mercy on him, died in Rajab 141 AH / August 765 AH.
In Rajab 101 AH / January 720 AD, Hazrat Umar bin Abdul Aziz, may God have mercy on him, died.
On 1 Rajab 150 AH / 2 August 765 AD, Imam Abu Hanifah, may God have mercy on him, died.
On 27 Rajab 182 AH / 22 May 798 AD, Qazi Al-Qadat Imam Abu Yusuf, whose name was Yaqub, died.
On 25 Rajab 183 AH / 8 August 799 AD, Imam Musa Kazim died and was buried in Baghdad. His grave and that of Imam Muhammad Taqi are under a dome in Baghdad known as Kazmin.
Imam Shafi'i died in Rajab 204 AH / December 819 AH.
On 18 Rajab 218 AH / 23 July 833 AD, Caliph Mamun al-Rashid died and Mutasimullah succeeded to the throne.
On 14 Rajab 256 AH / 4 June 870 AD, the Abbasid caliph Mehtadi Allah died and Motamid Ali Allah died and Mu'tazid Allah ascended the throne.

On 20 Rajab 479 AH / 10 December 1086 AH, a bloody battle took place between Yusuf bin Tashfin and Ralfanso VI in the field of Zalaqah. 20,000 Muslims defeated 60,000 Christians and Alfonso cut off his entire army and escaped with a few hundred men.
Syed Nazir Hussain Muhaddith Dehlavi died in Rajab 1320 AH / October 1902.
On 18 Rajab 1358 AH / 3 September 1939, the Second World War began.
Partition of India was announced on 14th Rajab 1366 AH / 3rd June 1947.
On 4 Rajab 1367 AH / 14 May 1948, Jews usurped Palestine and established Jamalia and Israel.
In Rajab 1384 AH / November 1964, Shah Faisal bin Abdulaziz was appointed as the king of Saudi Arabia.

On 22nd Rajab 1407 AH / 23rd March 1987, Allama Ihsan Elahi Zaheer was seriously injured in a bomb blast in Lachman Singh Fort (Lahore) and passed away on 29th Rajab / 30th March. He was buried in Baqi al-Ghurqad.

Fazail.text: The Prophet ﷺ used to fast almost the entire month of Shaban and only at the end did he break it for two days each. When he was asked the reason for this, he said: “People neglect this month. In this month (people's) deeds are presented before the Lord of the worlds and I like that when my deeds are presented before Allah, I am fasting at that time.''

Amaal.text: In this month, fasts should be observed frequently and other good deeds should be actively participated in because in this month the deeds are presented in the court of the Lord of the worlds.

Rasm.text: The fifteenth night of this month is known as "Shab Baraat". The excellence and greatness of this night is exaggerated, while its name "Shab Baraat" is not proven from the Qur'an and Hadith, nor is there any authentic tradition about its excellence. Shaykh Ibn Baz, may God have mercy on him, says that all the hadiths related to the virtue of Shab Baraat are weak, not even one of them is correct. (Fatawa Al-Lajnat Al-Daimima: 67/3), so all the following matters are not Masoon: Considering Shab-e-Bara'at better than Lailat-ul-Qadr or equal to it. On this night, perform the "Namaz-i-Bara'at", as well as read 6 rak'ahs for protection from calamities, a long life or indifference to people. Reciting Surah Yasin and supplicating: Allah, the Most Merciful, and the Most Merciful. To organize gatherings or gatherings for worship or sermons. Arranging halwa mandas and keeping the belief that the souls of the dead come on this night and the names of the dead are written down and given to Malik Alamut in the coming year. Also, going to graveyards, lighting lamps at mosques and special places, setting off fireworks and following other similar rituals and observing the fast of Rabindra Shaban.

Waqiat.text: In Shaban 2 AH / January 624 AD, Bait Allah (Makkah) was made the Qiblah instead of Bait al-Maqdis.
On 10 Sha'ban 2 AH / 6 Faravi 624 A.H., the order of obligatory fasting of Ramadan was revealed.
In Shaban 4 AH / January 626 AD, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went to Badr with 1500 Companions. It is called Second Battle of Badr.
In Shaban 5 AH / December 626 AD, Banu Mustalaq's invasion took place.
On 10 Shaban 12 AH / 20 October 633 AD, the Battle of Hasid was fought in Iraq in which the Iranians were defeated.
On 11 Sha'ban 12 AH / 21 October 633 AD, the Battle of Khanafs took place in Iraq in which the Muslims won.
On 19 Sha'ban 12 AH / 29 October 633 A.D., the Battle of the Messiah was fought in Iraq, in which the Muslims won.
On 8 Sha'ban 13 AH / 17 October 634 AD, Hazrat Abu Ubaid bin Masoud Al-Thaqafi (RA) won a victory against Jaban in the Battle of Namarq (Iraq).
On 12 Sha'ban 13 AH / 11 October 634 AD, Abu Ubayd (RA) defeated the Narsians in the Battle of Saqatiyah in Iraq.
On 7 Shaban 13 AH / 16 October 634 AD, Abu Ubayd Thaqafi (RA) defeated Galen at the place of "Baqsiyatha" in Iraq.

On 23 Sha'ban 13 AH / 22 October 634 AD, the Battle of Jasr took place between the armies of Hazrat Abu Ubaid bin Masoud Thaqafi (RA) and Rabhaman Jadawiya, in which six thousand Iranians were killed and four thousand Muslims were martyred, including Hazrat Abu Ubaid Thaqafi (RA). This was the first and last battle in which the Muslims were defeated by the Iranians.

On 13 to 16 Sha'ban 15 AH / 20 to 23 September 636, the battle of Qadsiyya was fought in which the Iranians suffered a crushing defeat. More than 30,000 Iranians were killed and 30,000 drowned in the Atiq canal, while 8,500 Muslims were martyred.
On 19 Sha'ban 23 AH / 8 June 644 A.D., boating began between the Egyptian "Fustat" and "Jar" ​​in the canal of Amir al-Mu'minin.
In Shaban 38 AH / December 658 AH, Hazrat Ali defeated the Kharijites at the place of Nahrwan.
In Shaban 93 AH / May 712 AH, Hazrat Anas bin Malik, the servant of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, died.
On Shaban 196 AH / April 812 AH, the son of Abbasid Caliph Mutasim Ballah, Harun was born, who became the Caliph in 227 AH under the name of Wasiq Ballah.
In Shaban 467 AH / March 1075, Sultan Malik Shah Seljuqi conquered Damascus and removed the addition of "Hay Ali Khair al-Amal" from Razan.

On 23 Sha'ban 492 AH / June 1099, the Christians captured Jerusalem after a 40-day siege. Seventy thousand Muslims were martyred in the Al-Aqsa Mosque and the Dome of the Rock and all the valuables and gold and silver lamps of the Al-Aqsa Mosque were looted.
In Shaban 848 AH / November 1444, the Ottoman Sultan Murad II took over the government again and defeated the European Union.
Maulana Bual Kalam Azad died on 2 Shaban 1377 AH/22 February 1958.

Fazail.text: Fasting in Ramadan is one of the five basic pillars of Islam (Bukhari: 8).
The Quran was revealed in Ramadan (Al-Baqarah: 185:2).
In Ramadan there is Lailatul-Qadr, whose worship is better than a thousand months. (Al-Qadr 3:97)
* Fasting in Ramadan with the intention of reward in the state of faith, previous sins are forgiven. (Bukhari: 38)
* In Ramadan, the gates of heaven are opened, the gates of hell are closed and the biggest devils are bound. (Bukhari: 1899)
Every night of Ramadan, Allah announces to excel in good deeds and refrain from sins. (Tirmidhi: 682)
Every night of Ramadan, Allah gives some people freedom from hell. (Tirmidhi: 682)
Performing Umrah in Ramadan is equivalent to performing Hajj with the Prophet ﷺ (Bukhari: 1863).

Amaal.text: It is obligatory on every sane, mature, healthy Muslim to fast the entire month of Ramadan. The Masnoon acts of Ramadan are as follows: Reciting Adhkar while fasting, praying in congregation and reciting the Qur'an frequently. Fasting by eating suhoor and breaking fast immediately after sunset. Organizing fast games. To organize Taraweeh prayer at night. Praying often, especially at the time of breaking the fast because it is a time of acceptance. Giving charity frequently. To organize Umrah. Doing Itikaaf in the last ten days of Ramadan and seeking Lailatul Qadr on odd nights. Women should not fast during certain days (menstruation).

Rasm.text: Arranging sermons and speeches and Shabina of the Qur'an on odd nights. Distributing sweets on the occasion of finishing the Qur'an, organizing long prayers and lighting lamps on mosques. Instead of reading Taraweeh for a whole month, reading Taraweeh for only three or five days and considering it enough to engage in business. Women's I'tikaf sitting in homes instead of mosques. All these actions are prohibited by the Qur'an and Sunnah.

Waqiat.date: 21st of Ramadan, the first prophetic month
Waqiat.text: On the 21st of Ramadan, the 1st of the Prophet, the revelation of revelation to the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, began.

Waqiat.date: Ramadan is the first prophetic month
Waqiat.text: May Hazrat Khadija (RA) bring Islam in the first month of Ramadan.
Hazrat Khadija died on 10th of Ramadan/May 619.
In the month of Ramadan 2 AH / February 624 AD, Sadaqah al-Fitr (Fitrana) became obligatory and Eid-ul-Fitr was ordered to be celebrated.
In Ramadan, 1st Hijri, March 623, Hazrat Hamza was made the Amir of 30 Muhajirin and sent to trace the Quraish caravan. It is called "Sariyah Al-Bahr".
On the 17th of Ramadan, 2H/14th March 624, the Battle of Badr took place in which 70 infidels including Abu Jahl were killed and 14 Muslims were martyred.
In Ramadan 8 AH / December 629, Makkah was conquered and the Raqaba was cleansed of idols.
Hazrat Fatima died in Ramadan 11 AH / November 632 AD.
In Ramadan 13 AH / November 634 AD, Hazrat Muthni bin Haritha won a glorious victory against the Iranians in the "Battle of Buayb".
On the 19th of Ramadan, 21 AH / 18 August 642 AD, Hazrat Amr bin As, may Allah be pleased with him, conquered Alexandria.
On the 21st of Ramadan 40 AH / January 661, Hazrat Ali was martyred and his son Hazrat Hasan became the Caliph.
Hazrat Aisha died in Ramadan 57 AH / July 677 AD.
On 28 Ramadan 92 AH / 19 July 711 AD, Tariq bin Ziyad won the battle of Wadi-e-Baqa against the tyrant King Roderick (Lazariq) of Spain and then conquered the areas up to Toledo (Toledo).

On the 10th of Ramadan 93 AH / June 712 AD, Muhammad bin Qasim, may Allah have mercy on him, defeated Raja Dahir of Sindh at Rawar. Raja Dahir was killed and Sindh became part of the Islamic Caliphate.
In Ramadan 93 AH / June 712, Musa bin Nusir landed on the coast of Gibraltar with his army and marched to Ashbelia via Madinah Shazuna and conquered it.
Azhar University (Cairo) was founded on the 7th of Ramadan 361 AH/2 June 972 AD during the reign of Ubaid (Fatimi) Caliph Al-Mu'izz.
Genghis Khan died in Ramadan 624 AH / August 1227 AD at the age of 73 after ruling for 25 years.

On the 15th of Ramadan 658 AH / 10 August 1260 AD, the Mamluks of Egypt humiliatingly defeated the Tatars at the Goliath Canal. The Islamic army was led by Ruknuddin Baybars. Thousands of Tatars died on the battlefield.
On 26th Ramadan 927 AH / 29th August 1521 AD, Sultan Suleiman the Magnificent conquered Belgrade (Serbia) and prayed in its largest church and converted it into a mosque.
In Ramadan 1332 AH / July 1914, the first world war began, which continued for four years.
Pakistan came into existence on 27th Ramadan 1366 AH / 14th August 1947.
On 10th of Ramadan 1393 AH / 6th October 1973, the third Arab-Israeli war began.
On the 1st of Ramadan 1395 AH / 7 September 1974, Mirzais were declared infidels by constitutional amendment in the Pakistani Parliament.
On the 5th of Ramadan 1426 AH / 8th October 2005, a deadly earthquake occurred in the northern parts of Pakistan and Kashmir.

Fazail.text: On the 1st of Shawwal, performing the Eid al-Fitr prayer outside in the open field. Before going out for the Eid prayer, perform the Fitrana first. Bathing for Eid, wearing new or washed clothes, arranging perfumes and reciting Takbirs until the Imam comes to the Masla. On the occasion of Eid, while meeting a Muslim brother, say Takbal Allah minna wa minkum and keep six fasts in the whole month.

Amaal.text: On the 1st of Shawwal, performing the Eid al-Fitr prayer outside in the open field. Before going out for the Eid prayer, perform the Fitrana first. Bathing for Eid, wearing new or washed clothes, arranging perfumes and reciting Takbirs until the Imam comes to the Masla. On the occasion of Eid, while meeting a Muslim brother, say Takbal Allah minna wa minkum and keep six fasts in the whole month.

Rasm.text: Due to the greatness and excellence of Shab-i-Eid, performing special worship in it. Performing Fitrana after Eid prayer. Performing the Eid prayer in the mosque without excuse. Keeping six fasts of Shawwal and then celebrating Eid on the eighth of Shawwal. Considering Shawwal as unlucky as some Arabs thought. It is important to hug after the Eid prayer. All these actions are prohibited by the Qur'an and Sunnah.

Waqiat.date: Shawwal 10th Prophet / May or June 619 AD
Waqiat.text: In Shawwal 10th of the Prophet / May or June 619 AD, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ visited Taif for the invitation to religion.
In Shawwal 10th of the Prophet / June 619 AD, the Prophet ﷺ married Hazrat Souda.
On the 11th of Shawwal/July 620 AD, the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) married Hazrat Aisha (RA).
In Shawwal 1st Hijri/April 623 A.H. Hazrat Aisha passed away in Madinah.
In Shawwal 1st Hijri / April 623 AD, the Prophet ﷺ sent Hazrat Ubaidah Bin Harith Radiyallahu Anhu as the Ameer of Syria along with sixty horsemen. It is popularly known as "Sariyyah Rabigh".
The first Eid-ul-Fitr was celebrated on 1st Shawwal 2H / 27th March 624 AD.
In Shawwal 2 AH / March 624 AD, the Prophet ﷺ made an unannounced attack in the area of ​​Banusulim with 200 people. This incident is known as "Ghazwa Bani Sulaym".
On 15th Shawwal 2H / 10th April 624 AD, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ besieged Banu Qaynaqa and exiled them from Medina on 30th Shawwal.
On 7 Shawwal 3 AH / 24 March 625 AD, the Uhud invasion took place. In this campaign, Wahzi bin Harb martyred Hazrat Hamza.

On 9 Shawwal 3 AH / 26 March 624 AD, the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, went to Hamra al-Asad with the Mujahideen of Uhud in pursuit of the Quraish. It is called "Ghazwa Hamra al-Assad".
In Shawwal 5 AH / Farui 627 AD, the Ahzab invasion took place.
In Shawwal 6 AH/Farwi 625 A.H., an army was sent to the tribe of Urina under the leadership of Hazrat Kurz bin Jabir Fahri (RA).
On 10 Shawwal 8 AH / 31 January 630 AD, the Hunain campaign took place.
In Shawwal 8 AH / January 630 AD, the Prophet ﷺ besieged Taif and returned to Madinah after fifteen and twenty days of siege.
On the 1st of Shawwal 43 AH / 6 January 664 AD, Hazrat Amr bin As (RA) died in Fustat.
In Shawwal 94 AH / July 713 AD, Musa bin Nasir, may God have mercy on him, conquered the regions up to Marda (Spain).
Hisham bin Abd al-Rahman was born in Shawwal 139 AH/ Farwi 757 AD, who became the ruler of Andalus in 172 AH/ 788 AD.
On 13 Shawwal 194 AH / 20 July 810 AD, Imam Abu Abdullah Muhammad bin Ismail Bukhari, may God have mercy on him, was born.
In Shawwal 223 AH / August 838 AD, Caliph Mutasim Bullah Abbasi conquered Amuria (Turkey) after a 55-day siege.
On 4 Shawwal 247 AH / 10 December 861 AD, Caliph Mutawakkil Ali Allah was assassinated by his son Muntasar Bullah and assumed the reins of government.
On 3 Shawwal 252 AH / 22 January 866 AH, the Abbasid Caliph Mustainullah was killed and Mu'tazibullah ascended the throne.
On 1st Shawwal 256 AH/31 August 870 AD, Imam Bukhari, may God have mercy on him, died in Khartang near Bukhara.
In Shawwal 339 AH / March 951 AD, Hajrasud was taken back from Qaramta and installed in its place in the Kaaba.

Sultan Malik Shah Seljuqi died on 15 Shawwal 485/4 November 1092 and was succeeded by his son Ruknuddin Barkiarq. Malik Shah had paved the way for Islamic power in the whole of Anatolia by inflicting a crushing defeat on Romanus Caesar Romanus in the Battle of Malazkurd (463 AH/1071 AD).

In Shawwal 582/December 1186, Sultan Shahabuddin Ghori captured Lahore and ended the two hundred year old Ghaznavid Empire. He defeated the Hindu Maharaja Prithviraj in the Second Battle of Tarayan (1192 AD) and started Islamic rule in North India. On the death of Shahabuddin Ghori in 1206 AD, his slave Qutbuddin Aybak became the first Muslim king of India.
On 9 Shawwal 926 AH / 22 September 1520, the Ottoman Caliph Salim I died and his son Sulaiman became the legal caliph.
On 15th Shawwal 1421 AH / 10th January 2001, the eminent religious scholar of Saudi Arabia Sheikh Saleh Uthaymeen, may God have mercy on him, died.

Fazail.text: Dhul-Qa'dah is one of the four sacred months. Apart from that, there is no proof of its virtues from the Qur'an and Hadith.

Amaal.text: In this month, no good deed has been made special with virtue, so the good deeds that are proven to be done in normal days should be done in this month as well.

Rasm.text: There is no known custom related to this month, however, non-Islamic customs of all kinds should be completely avoided.

Waqiat.text: In Dhul-Qa'da 10th of the Prophet/June 619 AD, the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, came to Makkah from Taif.
In Dhul-Qa'da 1st Hijri/May 623, the Prophet ﷺ made Hazrat Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas (RA) the Ameer of Syria and sent him along with 20 men to track down a Quraysh caravan, which is called "Siri Kharar".
In Zul-Qa'dah 5 AH / March 627, the invasion of Bani Quraiza took place. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ besieged Banu Quraiza for 25 days and on the 26th day they surrendered to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
In Zul-Qa'da 6 AH / March 628 AD, the peace of Hudaybiyah took place, which was declared as a victory by the Qur'an.
In Dhul-Qa'dah 7 AH/March 629, the Prophet ﷺ performed Umrah with 2000 Companions (excluding women and children).

In Dhul-Qa'da 8 AH / February 630 AD, the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, put on the Ihram of Umrah at Jarana (near Wadi Hunain) and performed Umrah. It is called ``Umra Ji'arana''.
On 8 Dhul-Qa'dah 12 AH / 14 January 634 AD, Hazrat Hamza bin Malik came to Madinah with 3000 people and on 15th Dhul-Qa'dah he left for Syria with his party.
On 15 Dhul-Qa'dah 12 AH/23 January 634 A.D., there was a "battle of war" between Hazrat Khalid bin Waleed, may God be pleased with him, and the Bedouin tribes loyal to the Romans, Iranians, and Ran.
In Dhul-Qa'da 13 AH / December 634, Yazdgerd III was crowned on the throne of Iran, who proved to be the last king of the Sassanid Empire.

On 1st Dhul-Qa'dah 15 AH / 7 December 636 A.D., the Iranian ruler Busbahri was defeated in Burs (Iraq) against Zuhra bin Hawiyah (RA) and fled to Babylon. On the 3rd of Dhul-Qa'dah, the chiefs of Buras made peace on the payment of jizya to Zahra.
On 13 Dhul-Qa'dah 15 AH / 17 December 636 AD, Nabata bin Jashim (RA) killed Shahryar in a duel with Shahryar in Kusthi (Iraq).
On 28th Dhul-Qa'dah 15 AH / 1st January 637 AD, Shirzad, the ruler of Sabat, reached an agreement on the payment of Jizya.
The battle of Jalula was fought on 1st Dhul-Qa'dah 16 AH / 24 November 637 AD, in which Hazrat Utbah bin Ghazwan (RA) killed the Iranian general Mehran and won.
On 28 Dhul-Qa'dah 20 AH / 8 November 641 A.D., Cyrus the Great made peace with the Muslims on a mortgage with the approval of Emperor Constantine.
In Zul-Qa'da 21 AH / October 642 AD, Cyrus handed over the keys of Alexandria to Hazrat Amr bin As (RA) according to the Treaty of Babylon. Thus the victory of Egypt was completed.
On 25 Dhul-Qa'da 206 AH / 21 April 822 AH, the Amir of Andalus Haqm bin Hisham died at the age of 52 years and his son Abd al-Rahman II (Awsat) ascended the throne.
On 18 Dhul-Qa'da 289 AH / 24 October 902 AD, Ibrahim bin Ahmed Aghbali, the conqueror of southern Italy, died in front of "Cosenza" and the Muslims returned to Sicily (Sicily).
In Dhul-Qa'da 416 AH / January 1026, Sultan Mahmud Ghaznavi conquered Somnath (Gujarat Kathiawar) and broke the idol named Somnath and was called Ram Mahmud idol breaker.

In Dhul-Qa'da 450 AH/December 1058, Basasiri captured Baghdad in the absence of Sultan Tughril Beg Saljuqi and delivered the Fatimid sermon and ordered the addition of "Hai Ali Khairul Amal" in the Adhan.
On 18 Dhul-Qa'dah 602 AH / 27 June 1206 AD, Qutb-ud-Din Aibak ascended the throne as the first Muslim king of India.
On 1st Zul-Qa'da 656 AH / 30 October 1258 AD, Halaku Khan conquered the Fort of Alamut and arrested the king of the Ismailis, Ruknuddin Khurshah.
Shaykh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allah have mercy on him, died in the prison of Damascus on 1st Dhul-Qa'da 728 AH / 8th September 1328 AH.
On 23 Dhul-Qa'dah 798 AH / 9 September 1396 AD, Ottoman Sultan Bayezid Yildirim defeated the United Christian Army of Europe in the battle of Nicopolis (Bulgaria).
On 12-10 Zul-Qa'da 852 AH / 19-17 January 1448, Sultan Murad II defeated the European Union in the Battle of Kosovo.
On 27 Dhul-Qa'dah 898 AH / 9 September 1493, the governor of Bosnia, Jacob Pasha, conquered Slovenia and Croatia. 5,700 crusaders were killed and 25,000 were captured in the Battle of Croatia.
On 20 Dhul-Qa'da 932 AH / 29 August 1526, the Ottomans won against the Hungarian army in the Battle of Mohacs and the Hungarian state became part of the Ottoman Empire.
On 29th Zul-Qa'da 1213 AH / 4th May 1799, the British martyred Tipu Sultan, Crown Prince of Mysore, in Seringapatam.
On 24th Zul-Qa'da 1246 AH / 6th May 1837, Syed Ahmad Barelvi and Shah Ismail, may Allah have mercy on him, were martyred while fighting the Sikh army at Balakot.
On 8 Dhul-Qa'dah 1369 AH/ 13 September 1948, India occupied Hyderabad Deccan, the largest Muslim state in the subcontinent.
On 26th Dhul-Qa'dah 1414 AH / 5th May 1994, "Dar-ul-Salaam", an international organization for the publication of the Book and Sunnah, started its publishing work in Pakistan (Lahore).

Fazail.text: Hajj is performed in the month of Dhul-Hijjah, which is the fifth pillar of Islam.
* On the day of Arafah (9th of Dhu al-Hijjah), the Nafil fast is observed, by which the sins of one year past and one year to come are forgiven. (Muslim: 1162)
On the 10th of Dhu al-Hijjah, the second Islamic Eid "Eid al-Adha" is celebrated and Muslims perform sacrifices by the order of Allah.
* The first decade of Dhu al-Hijjah is of great greatness and excellence. No good deed in this decade is as pleasing to Allah as it is in any other day. (Bukhari: 969)

Amaal.text: In the first decade of Dhu al-Hijjah, special arrangements should be made for good deeds, prayer, charity, remembrance, fasting and recitation of the Qur'an. Hajj to Bait Allah if you can afford it. Reciting Takbirs from the sighting of the moon of Dhu al-Hijjah to the evening of 13 Dhu al-Hijjah. Fasting on the Day of Arafah, i.e. 9 Dhu al-Hijjah. This forgives the sins of one year past and one year to come. On the 10th of Dhu al-Hijjah, performing the Eid al-Adha prayer outside in the open field and then performing the sacrifice. Congratulating a Muslim brother by saying ``Taqibul Allah minna wa minkum.'' If you want to make a sacrifice, cut your nails and head hair before seeing the moon of Dhu al-Hijjah, and then cut it after the Eid prayer. It should be noted that one sacrifice is enough for the whole house and sacrifice can be done up to 10 to 13 Dhu al-Hijjah. Therefore, it is necessary for all family members to cut their hair and nails before seeing the moon. Do not eat or drink or fast on the days of Tashriq (13, 12, 11 Dhu al-Hijjah).

Rasm.text: Considering the night of Eid as great and excellent, perform special worship on this night. Performing the Eid prayer in the mosque without excuse. It is considered necessary to do Manaqah after the Eid prayer. Reciting Takbeerat together. Sacrifice before the Eid prayer (in towns, villages) and considering it as makruh to sacrifice at night. Fasting on any of the days of Eid al-Adha and the days of Tashreeq. All these actions are prohibited by the Qur'an and Sunnah.

Waqiat.text: In Dhul-Hijjah 12 Nabi/July 621, the first pledge of allegiance was given to Aqaba.
In Dhul-Hijjah 13th of the Prophet/June 622 A.D., the second pledge of allegiance was given to Aqaba and the Prophet ﷺ was invited to migrate to Madinah.
In Dhul-Hijjah 2 AH / May 624 AD, the invasion of Suwayq took place.
In Dhul-Hijjah 8 AH / March 630 A.D., the Prophet's son Ibrahim was born to Hazrat Maria Qobtiya (RA).
In Dhul-Hijjah 9 AH / March 631 AD, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddiq (RA) as the Ameer of Hajj.
In Dhu al-Hijjah 10 AH / Farwi 632 AH, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed Hajj with many companions, which is called Hajj al-Wadaa.
In Dhul-Hijjah 12 AH / February 634 AD, the son-in-law of the Messenger of Allah, peace be upon him, Hazrat Abul As bin Rabi, may Allah be pleased with him, died.
On 24 Dhu al-Hijjah 12 AH / 1 March 634 AD, the battle of Arabah and Dathan (in Palestine) was fought.
On 3 Dhu al-Hijjah 15 AH / 6 January 637 AD, the dangerous battle of Sabat took place in which Hashim bin Utbah (RA) killed the lion of Kasra and defeated the royal guard.
In Dhul-Hijjah 17 AH / December 638 A.H., Al-Jiziya (the area between the Tigris and the Euphrates) was conquered by Hazrat Ayaz bin Ghannam.
On 29 Dhu al-Hijjah 20 AH / 7 December 641 AD, Hazrat Zubair bin Awam (RA) jumped over the wall of Babylon (Egypt) fort and the fort was conquered.
On 26 Dhu al-Hijjah 23 AH / 31 October 644 A.H., Abu Lulu Firoz Majusi wounded Hazrat Umar bin Khattab (RA) with a poisoned dagger.
On 18 Dhu al-Hijjah 35 AH / 17 June 656 AD, Hazrat Uthman bin Affan (RA) was martyred in a general riot.
On 13 Dhu al-Hijjah 136 AH / 28 May 754 AD, the founder of the Abbasid Caliphate, Abul Abbas Abdullah al-Saffah, died.
On 24 Dhu al-Hijjah 232 AH/19 July 847 AD, the Abbasid Caliph Wasiq Bullah died and his brother Mutawakkul Ali Allah succeeded to the throne.

On 9 Dhu al-Hijjah 318 AH / 25 December 930 AD, Abu Tahir, the chief of Qaramta, massacred the pilgrims in Makkah and dumped the dead bodies in Chah-Zam-Zam. He smashed the Black Stone with a hammer and separated it from the wall of the Kaaba and left it there for 11 days. For 20 years, Hajrasood remained in the possession of Qaramta.
On 18 Dhu al-Hijjah 352 AH/December 963, Mu'az-ud-Daula Bayihi ordered Eid to be observed in Baghdad and named it "Eid Ghadir Kham".

On 3 Dhu al-Hijjah 362 AH/December 973, the great Muslim scientist Abu Rayhan Al-Biruni was born in the town of Barun near the city of Khiva (Khwarizm) in Central Asia. His book Il Hind, based on tourism in India, is very popular.
In Dhul-Hijjah 852 AH / January 1449 AH, Sahih Al-Bukhari, Hafiz Ibn Hajar Asqalani, may God have mercy on him, died.
On 29 Dhu al-Hijjah 922 AH / 22 January 1517 AD, Ottoman Sultan Salim I defeated the army of Mamluk Egypt in Redania.
On 28 Dhul-Hijjah 978 AH / 24 May 1571, Tatar and Ottoman armies entered Moscow and Khan Crimea returned from Moscow with 15,000 prisoners.
On 8 Dhu al-Hijjah 1418 AH / 6 April 1998, Pakistan successfully tested the Ghori missile.
